mnemonics.title: BUS FUNCTIONS
mnemonics.content: DAC: Data, Address, Control - THE THREE PILLARS OF BUS COMMUNICATION

mnemonics.title: ARBITRATION METHODS
mnemonics.content: DPI: Daisy chain, Polling, Independent request - THREE METHODS TO RESOLVE BUS CONFLICTS

mnemonics.title: SYNCHRONOUS VS ASYNCHRONOUS
mnemonics.content: SADA: Synchronous uses A.clock, Asynchronous uses D.ata handshaking - SADA HELPS YOU REMEMBER

mnemonics.title: ADDRESS BUS DIRECTION
mnemonics.content: A TO M: Address bus goes TO Memory - A2M HELPS REMEMBER IT IS UNIDIRECTIONAL FROM CPU

mnemonics.title: DATA BUS BIDIRECTIONAL
mnemonics.content: DAD: Data bus can go FROM and TO CPU - DAD SHOWS IT IS BIDIRECTIONAL

acronyms.usage: The main processor that controls bus operations and initiates data transfers
Allows I/O devices to transfer data directly to/from memory without CPU intervention, using bus mastering
Standard expansion bus for connecting peripheral devices
Standard serial bus for connecting external devices
Early standard bus for IBM compatible computers
Enhanced version of ISA with 32-bit data bus

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a highway: a 8-bit bus is like a 1-lane road, 16-bit is 2 lanes, 32-bit is 4 lanes, 64-bit is 8 lanes - more lanes means more cars (bits) can travel simultaneously
Think of address bus as house delivery addresses written on letters - the address tells WHERE to deliver, not WHAT is inside
Think of data bus as the delivery truck carrying the actual package/contents between locations
Imagine a round-table discussion where multiple people want to speak - arbitration is like the chair person deciding who speaks next based on priority
Synchronous is like musicians following a conductor's beat (clock), asynchronous is like two people using hand signals to coordinate passing something

analogies.analogy: A bus is like the nervous system of a computer - just as nerves carry signals throughout your body, a bus carries data and control signals between computer components
Address bus is like the destination on a shipping label, data bus is like the actual package being shipped - you need both to complete a delivery
Bus arbitration is like traffic lights at an intersection - when multiple roads meet, something must decide which direction gets to go first
Bus width is like the number of checkout counters at a store - more counters (wider bus) means more customers (data) can be served at the same time

Synchronous bus is like people doing a synchronized swim routine - everyone moves on the same beat. Asynchronous is like two people passing a ball while talking - they coordinate through signals, not a common timer
